book_claims.claim: Omega-3 and omega-6 are essential fatty acids the human body cannot generate; essential for normal cell and organ function
book_claims.mechanism: Structural component of cell membranes (phospholipids); precursors to anti-inflammatory eicosanoids

book_claims.claim: Omega-3 multivitamin supplements are recommended at least twice during the preparatory week before fasting-mimicking diet
book_claims.mechanism: EPA and DHA support cell membrane function, reduce inflammation, and support cardiovascular and neurological health during fasting preparation

book_claims.claim: Deficiencies in omega-3 fatty acids have been implicated in brain aging and dementias
book_claims.mechanism: Omega-3 fatty acids are essential components of neuronal cell membranes and have anti-inflammatory effects in the brain

book_claims.claim: Intake of mono- and polyunsaturated fats is associated with reduced cardiovascular disease
book_claims.mechanism: Polyunsaturated fats favorably affect lipid profiles and reduce inflammation

supplementary_research.finding: VITAL trial (PMID 31567591): marine omega-3 supplementation (1 g/day) significantly reduced major cardiovascular events by 28% in adults without prior CVD over 5 years; heart attack risk reduced by 28%

Omega-3 EPA/DHA intake is inversely associated with cognitive decline and Alzheimer's disease risk in multiple prospective cohort studies (PMID 28900017); DHA is the predominant fatty acid in neuronal membranes

Fish-derived EPA and DHA consistently reduce triglycerides by 15–30%, improve endothelial function, and modulate inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-α); supplementation with 1–3 g/day is well supported across hundreds of RCTs

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: varies by source; ~1.5–2 g per 100 g salmon
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Marine EPA is highly bioavailable; triglyceride form (fish) slightly better absorbed than ethyl ester supplements

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: varies by source; ~1–1.5 g per 100 g salmon
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Critical for neuronal membrane fluidity; preferentially incorporated into brain and retina; absorption enhanced by dietary fat

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: ~22 g per 100 g flaxseed; ~6.8 g per 100 g walnuts
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Plant-based omega-3; conversion to EPA/DHA is inefficient (<10%); dietary fat context improves conversion marginally

synergies.description: Anchovies are a primary dietary source of EPA/DHA; consuming whole fish provides omega-3s alongside vitamin D and calcium, amplifying bone and cardiovascular benefits
Sardines and anchovies together fulfill the Longevity Diet's 2–3 weekly fish servings for optimal EPA/DHA intake with low mercury exposure
Omega-3s and vitamin D cooperatively regulate inflammatory gene expression; co-deficiency in both amplifies cardiovascular and cognitive risk
